title: Comparing probabilities
description: Practice expressing probabilities in different forms (fractions, decimals, and percents).

assessment_items.question_content:
The probability of Sandy flipping a coin twice and getting heads both times is $0.25$. The probability of John's spinner stopping on the color red is $\dfrac14$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's convert the probabilities to decimal form.

> $\text{P(Sandy flips two heads}) = \blue{0.25}$

> $\text{P(John's spinner stops on red}) = \dfrac14 = \pink{0.25}$

>$\blue{0.25} = \pink{0.25}$

Neither. Both events are equally likely.

choices.content: Sandy flips a coin twice and gets heads both times.
John's spinner stops on the color red.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Harry and Larry are having a barbecue dinner. The probability of Harry staining his shirt during the dinner is $\dfrac15$. The probability of Larry staining his shirt during the dinner is $15\%$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Harry stains his shirt during the dinner}) = \dfrac15= \pink{0.2} $

> $\text{P(Larry stains his shirt during the dinner}) = 15\%=\blue{0.15} $

>$\pink{0.2}>\blue{0.15}$

It is more likely that Harry stains his shirt  during the dinner.

choices.content: Harry stains his shirt during the dinner.
Larry stains his shirt during the dinner.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ahmed and Gavin are playing both chess and checkers. The probability of Ahmed winning the chess game is $45\%$. The probability of Ahmed winning the checkers game is $0.36$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Ahmed wins the chess game}) = 45\% =\pink{0.45}$

> $\text{P(Ahmed wins the checkers game}) = \blue{0.36} $

>$\pink{0.45}>\blue{0.36}$

It is more likely that Ahmed wins the chess game.

choices.content: Ahmed wins the chess game.
Ahmed wins the checkers game.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Paulina plays both volleyball and soccer. The probability of her getting injured playing volleyball is $0.1$. The probability of her getting injured playing soccer is $\dfrac{1}{10}$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Injured playing volleyball}) = \pink{0.1} $

> $\text{P(Injured playing soccer}) = \dfrac{1}{10}=\blue{0.1} $

>$\pink{0.1}=\blue{0.1}$

Neither. Both events are equally likely.

choices.content: Paulina gets injured playing volleyball.
Paulina gets injured playing soccer.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Simone is stranded on an island. The probability of her finding safety by waiting for help is $\dfrac14$. The probability of her finding safety by leaving in her raft is $0.32$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Safety by waiting for help}) = \dfrac14 =\pink{0.25}$

> $\text{P(Safety by leaving in her raft}) =\blue{0.32} $

>$\blue{0.32}>\pink{0.25}$

It is more likely that Simone finds safety by leaving in her raft.

choices.content: Simone finds safety by waiting for help.
Simone finds safety by leaving in her raft.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ilke is deciding whether to take his girlfriend on a dinner date or on a movie date. The probability of his having a successful dinner date is $55\%$. The probability of his having a successful movie date is $0.45$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Successful dinner date}) = 55\%=\pink{0.55} $

> $\text{P(Successful movie date}) = \blue{0.45} $

>$\pink{0.55}>\blue{0.45}$

It is more likely that Ilke has a successful dinner date.

choices.content: Ilke has a successful dinner date.
Ilke has a successful movie date.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
The probability of it snowing in Eastern Canada tomorrow is $0.4$. The probability of it snowing in Western Canada tomorrow is $\dfrac25$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Snows in Eastern Canada tomorrow}) = \pink{0.4} $

> $\text{P(Snows in Western Canada tomorrow}) = \dfrac25=\blue{0.4} $

>$\pink{0.4}=\blue{0.4}$

Neither. Both events are equally likely.

choices.content: It snows in Eastern Canada tomorrow.
It snows in Western Canada tomorrow.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
The probability of Jamie making money investing in bonds is $\dfrac{1}{20}$. The probability of Jamie making money investing in stocks is $82\%$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Make money investing in bonds}) = \dfrac{1}{20} =\pink{0.05}$

> $\text{P(Make money investing in stocks}) = 82\%=\blue{0.82} $

>$\blue{0.82}>\pink{0.05}$

It is more likely that Jamie makes money investing in stocks.

choices.content: Jamie makes money investing in bonds.
Jamie makes money investing in stocks.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Juanita and Nina are bowling together. The probability of Juanita getting a strike next game is $24\%$. The probability of Nina getting a strike next game is $0.17$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Juanita gets a strike next game}) = 24\%=\pink{0.24} $

> $\text{P(Nina get a strike next game}) = \blue{0.17} $

>$\pink{0.24}>\blue{0.17}$

It is more likely that Juanita gets a strike next game.

choices.content: Juanita gets a strike next game.
Nina gets a strike next game.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Both Felipe and Enzo forgot to study for their science test. The probability of Felipe passing the test is $0.19$. The probability of Enzo passing the test is $\dfrac14$.

**Which of these events is more likely?**
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Felipe passes the test}) = \pink{0.19} $

> $\text{P(Enzo passes the test}) = \dfrac14 =\blue{0.25}$

>$\blue{0.25} > \pink{0.19}$

It is more likely that Enzo passes the test.

choices.content: Felipe passes the test.
Enzo passes the test.
Neither. Both events are equally likely.

assessment_items.question_content:
Erin, Elizabeth, and Anna are playing a game. Their probabilities of winning the game are as follows:

> $\text{P(Erin wins}) = 0.3$

> $\text{P(Anna wins}) = \dfrac12$

> $\text{P(Elizabeth wins}) = 20\%$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Erin wins}) = \pink{0.3}$

> $\text{P(Anna wins}) = \dfrac12=\blue{0.5}$

> $\text{P(Elizabeth wins}) = 20\% = \purple{0.2}$

>$\purple{0.2}<\pink{0.3}<\blue{0.5}$

The events are listed below from least likely to most likely. 

> $\text{Elizabeth wins, Erin wins, Anna wins}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Victor has been wrongfully accused of helping a gorilla escape from the zoo. The probabilities of the court rulings are as follows:

> $\text{P(Guilty}) = 0.25$

> $\text{P(Innocent}) = 55\%$

> $\text{P(Retrial}) = \dfrac15$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Guilty}) = \pink{0.25}$

> $\text{P(Innocent}) = 55\%=\blue{0.55}$

> $\text{P(Retrial}) = \dfrac15=\purple{0.2}$

>$\purple{0.2}<\pink{0.25}<\blue{0.55}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Retrial, Guilty, Innocent}$

assessment_items.question_content:
I am Pat, Good Legs Lance, and Colorado Bronze are horses in a horse race. Their probabilities of winning the race are as follows:

> $\text{P(I Am Pat wins}) = \dfrac{3}{10}$

> $\text{P(Good Legs Lance wins}) = 0.6$

> $\text{P(Colorado Bronze wins}) = 10\%$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(I Am Pat wins}) = \dfrac{3}{10}=\pink{0.3}$

> $\text{P(Good Legs Lance wins}) = \blue{0.6}$

> $\text{P(Colorado Bronze wins}) = 10\%=\purple{0.1}$

>$\purple{0.1}<\pink{0.3}<\blue{0.6}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Colorado Bronze wins, I Am Pat wins, Good Legs Lance wins}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Marvin Martian, Aly Alien, and Hank Human are all looking for a hidden spaceship. Their probabilities of finding the spaceship first are as follows:

> $\text{P(Marvin wins}) = 30\%$

> $\text{P(Aly wins}) = 0.2$

> $\text{P(Hank wins}) = \dfrac12$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Marvin wins}) = 30\%=\pink{0.3}$

> $\text{P(Aly wins}) = \blue{0.2}$

> $\text{P(Hank wins}) = \dfrac12=\purple{0.5}$

>$\blue{0.2}<\pink{0.3}<\purple{0.5}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Aly wins, Marvin wins, Hank wins}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Carl is wondering if the train he is riding home from school will leave early, on time, or late. The probabilities are as follows:

> $\text{P(Train leaves early}) = 25\%$

> $\text{P(Train leaves on time}) = 0.35$

> $\text{P(Train leaves late}) = \dfrac25$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Early}) = 25\%=\pink{0.25}$

> $\text{P(On time}) = \blue{0.35}$

> $\text{P(Late}) = \dfrac25= \purple{0.4}$

>$\pink{0.25}<\blue{0.35}<\purple{0.4}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Train leaves early, Train leaves on time, Train leaves late}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Vera ordered a soup, salad, and sandwich to share with her friends.  The probabilities of each item being ready first are as follows:

> $\text{P(Soup ready first}) = \dfrac15$

> $\text{P(Salad ready first}) = 0.45$

> $\text{P(Sandwich ready first}) = 35\%$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Soup ready first}) = \dfrac15= \pink{0.2}$

> $\text{P(Salad ready first}) = \blue{0.45}$

> $\text{P(Sandwich ready first}) = 35\%=\purple{0.35}$

>$\pink{0.2}<\purple{0.35}<\blue{0.45}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Soup ready first, Sandwich ready first, Salad ready first}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Tina rides the bus to school, Theo bikes to school, and Tony walks to school.  Their probabilities of being the first one to school are as follows:

> $\text{P(Tina is first}) = 0.05$

> $\text{P(Theo is first}) = 85\%$

> $\text{P(Tony is first}) = \dfrac{1}{10}$


**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Tina is first}) =\pink{0.05}$

> $\text{P(Theo is first}) = 85\%=\blue{0.85}$

> $\text{P(Tony is first}) = \dfrac{1}{10}=\purple{0.1}$

>$\pink{0.05}<\purple{0.1}<\blue{0.85}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Tina is first, Tony is first, Theo is first}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Gustavo is the league's home run leader.  The probabilities for his total number of home runs for the season are as follows:

> $\text{P(0-19 home runs}) = \dfrac14$

> $\text{P(20-39 home runs}) = 45\%$

> $\text{P(40+ home runs}) = 0.3$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(0-19 home runs}) = \dfrac14=\pink{0.25}$

> $\text{P(20-39 home runs}) = 45\%=\blue{0.45}$

> $\text{P(40+ home runs}) = \purple{0.3}$

>$\pink{0.25}<\purple{0.3}<\blue{0.45}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{0-19 home runs, 40+ home runs, 20-39 home runs}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Ella, Danila, and Sophia are competing in an eating contest. Their probabilities of winning the contest are as follows:

> $\text{P(Ella wins}) = 0.75$

> $\text{P(Danila wins}) = 5\%$

> $\text{P(Sophia wins}) = \dfrac15$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Ella wins}) = \pink{0.75}$

> $\text{P(Danila wins}) = 5\%=\blue{0.05}$

> $\text{P(Sophia wins}) = \dfrac15=\purple{0.2}$

>$\blue{0.05}<\purple{0.2}<\pink{0.75}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Danila wins, Sophia wins, Ella wins}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Rory, Phil, and Rickie are competing in a golf match. Their probabilities of winning the match are as follows:

> $\text{P(Rory wins}) = 20\%$

> $\text{P(Phil wins}) = 0.05$

> $\text{P(Rickie wins}) = \dfrac34$

**Put the following events in order from least to most likely.**

[[☃ sorter 1]]
hints: Events with greater probability are more likely.

Let's put the probabilities in decimal form.

> $\text{P(Rory wins}) = 20\%=\pink{0.2}$

> $\text{P(Phil wins}) = \blue{0.05}$

> $\text{P(Rickie wins}) = \dfrac34=\purple{0.75}$

>$\blue{0.05}<\pink{0.2}<\purple{0.75}$

The events are listed below from least to most likely. 

> $\text{Phil wins, Rory wins, Rickie wins}$
